trigger: glob
description: "Enforces rules for the Canvas2D rendering pipeline."

<!-- Generated by /arch-review | Last updated: 2026-06-18 -->

# Canvas Rendering Invariants

These rules enforce the rendering logic constraints for the HTML5 Canvas 2D pipeline.

## Render Logic

### Mandatory Patterns
- **DO**: Divide stroke widths by `viewport.zoom` to maintain scale-independent visual thickness across all zoom levels.
- **DO**: Draw screen-space overlays (e.g., crosshairs, snap indicators, UCS) AFTER calling `ctx.restore()` to revert world-space transforms.
- **DO**: Account for `viewport.zoom` when performing hit-testing (e.g., handle radius in screen space vs world space).
- **DO**: Split entity rendering logic into focused, single-responsibility files (e.g., `draw-wall.ts`, `draw-dimension.ts`) rather than a monolithic file.

### Prohibited Patterns
- ❌ Do not apply CSS `zoom` to the `<canvas>` element itself, as it causes blurring and breaks coordinate calculations.
- ❌ Do not mix rendering logic with business logic or state mutation.

## Known Violations (as of 2026-06-18)

- **[CRIT-003]** `src/canvas/draw-helpers.ts`: Massive file size and SRP violation. All entity drawing logic is jammed into one 1033-line file.
- **[CRIT-007]** `src/canvas/canvas-component.tsx`: Monolithic Canvas component combining rendering orchestrations and event handlers.
- **[WARN-002]** `src/canvas/draw-helpers.ts`: Duplicated rendering logic between Canvas2D and SVG export.
